title: DDL Export Enhancements with Field Filtering and Table Name Persistence
story: As a data migration specialist, I want to choose which fields to include in my DDL export and have the system remember my table names, so that I can efficiently generate accurate DDL scripts across multiple sessions.
description:
Enhance the DDL export feature based on user feedback with three key improvements:

1. **Field filtering options**: Three export scopes — "All Fields" for the object, "Migration Fields" (only visible on the migration screen, includes fields marked "Yes"), and "Reporting Fields" (only visible on the dictionary screen, includes fields marked "Yes").

2. **Script type options**: In addition to the existing "Create Table (DDL)", add an "Add Fields" option that generates ALTER TABLE statements (e.g., `ALTER TABLE Leads ADD Field1 Type, Field2 Type`).

3. **Persist table name**: Store the output database table name and target database for each Salesforce object. When the user returns to the DDL screen, pre-fill their previously entered table names. Updates are saved when changed.

The field filter options are contextually shown — Migration Fields only appears on the migration screen, Reporting Fields only on the dictionary screen.
acceptance_criteria: User can select 'All Fields', 'Migration Fields', or 'Reporting Fields' as the export scope
Migration Fields option is only visible on the migration screen
Reporting Fields option is only visible on the dictionary screen
User can choose between 'Create Table' and 'Add Fields' script types
Add Fields script type generates ALTER TABLE statements
Output table name is persisted per Salesforce object and pre-filled on return visits
Target database selection is persisted per Salesforce object
Table name changes are saved when the user updates them
ux_changes: DDL export modal updated with field scope dropdown (All/Migration/Reporting), script type selector (Create Table/Add Fields), and pre-filled table name input that persists across sessions.
middleware_changes: Logic to filter fields based on selected scope (all, migration-flagged, reporting-flagged). Prompt construction updated to support ALTER TABLE syntax for the Add Fields option.
data_model_changes: New fields on the Salesforce object record to store the user's output table name and target database type.
api_changes: Updated DDL generation endpoint to accept scope and script type parameters. New endpoint to save/retrieve persisted table name and database type per object.
implementation_notes: Table name and database type are stored at the scorecard object level. The persist feature saves per Salesforce object, not per user. Initially there was a regression that broke existing functionality when adding the new fields, which was quickly resolved. Token limits from the OpenAI API caused incomplete results for large objects, which was addressed by adjusting max_tokens.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the field filtering and script type changes but needed clarification on the persist feature. Confirmed that table name and database type should be stored at the Salesforce object level. An OpenAI token limit issue caused incomplete DDL output for large objects, which was resolved.
